name: Siete Hermanos Trail
name_original: Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña — Running circular hasta el Mirador del Avión
description: Jog the moderate Siete Hermanos Trail in Sierra Espuña, a 4.9-mile route with 804 feet of gain and panoramic views from the Airplane Viewpoi
region_intro: Jogging around National Park Sierra Espuña offers a diverse range of environments, from dense pine forests to high-mountain scenery and unique geological formations. The region is characterized by an uneven relief with an average altitude of 800 meters, featuring peaks up to 1,583 meters and dramatic ravines. Extensive reforestation efforts have created shaded trails, while striking geological features like the Barrancos de Gebas present a "lunar" or "badlands" landscape. Best jogging routes around National Park Sierra Espuña The most popular jogging route is Siete Hermanos Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is View of the Dry Lake loop from Moriana, a difficult 16.3 miles (26.2 km) path. This route traverses diverse landscapes, providing a challenging long-distance running experience. Local runners also love the Collado del Pilón Viewpoint – Collado Bermejo loop from Bar Restaurante Los Donceles, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail leading through high-mountain scenery, often completed in about 4 hours 42 minutes. This difficult route includes panoramic views from viewpoints like Collado Bermejo. Jogging around National Park Sierra Espuña is defined by dense pine forests, varied topography, and unique geological formations such as ravines and "badlands" landscapes. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate jogs to challenging long-distance runs. The routes in National Park Sierra Espuña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 350 runners have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra Espuña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: National Park Sierra Espuña offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 35 different trails to explore. These range from moderate jogs to challenging long-distance runs, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Sierra Espuña?
A: You'll encounter diverse terrain, from dense pine forests providing shaded paths to high-mountain scenery with peaks up to 1,583 meters. The park also features unique geological formations like dramatic ravines and areas with a 'lunar' or 'badlands' landscape, such as the Barrancos de Gebas. The varied topography ensures an engaging experience for runners.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints to see while running?
A: Yes, the park is rich in natural beauty and offers several breathtaking viewpoints. You can enjoy panoramic views from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt;, which overlooks the Valley of the Espuña River, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Airplane Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Other striking geological formations include the Paredes de Leyva cliff faces and the Cuchillos de El Bosque.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: The running routes in National Park Sierra Espuña are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 350 runners have used komoot to explore the park's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained trails.

Q: Are there options for experienced runners seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. The park is well-suited for experienced runners, with 33 of its 39 routes classified as difficult. For a significant challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31414663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Dry Lake loop from Moriana&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.2 km path, or the even longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21848561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Pilón Viewpoint – Collado Bermejo loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 33.0 km and includes substantial elevation gain.

Q: Can I see any wildlife while running in Sierra Espuña?
A: Yes, National Park Sierra Espuña is a protected area teeming with wildlife. While running, you might spot the endemic Espuña squirrel, mouflons, wild boars, and various birds of prey such as eagle owls and golden eagles. The park is home to 185 recorded animal species, offering a chance to connect with nature.

Q: Are there any historical sites along the running routes?
A: Yes, some routes pass by the fascinating Pozos de Nieve (Snow Pits). These 16th-century circular structures, located at 1,400 meters altitude, were historically used to store snow for ice production and offer a unique glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sierra Espuña?
A: Sierra Espuña offers good running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. During summer, the dense pine forests offer shaded trails, which can be a relief from the heat, while winter runs might require appropriate gear for higher altitudes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: Many of the running routes in Sierra Espuña are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900735385/siete-hermanos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siete Hermanos Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6890841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón de Alhama Summit loop from Zona de Acampada La Perdiz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes with views of El Peñón?
A: Yes, you can find running routes that offer stunning views of El Peñón. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21848239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of El Peñón loop from Cabezo del Mortero&lt;/a&gt;, which provides excellent vistas of this prominent natural landmark.
tips: Panoramic point with very beautiful views of the entire Sierra Espuña regional park and the Huerta de Murcia valley. A very picturesque place that can be reached via several trails.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Siete Hermanos Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is known for its very technical and rugged terrain. Expect a challenging run with lots of rocks, roots, and sections that may require scrambling. It traverses a ridge with continuous climbs and descents, making it a demanding experience.

faqs.question: How difficult is this trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: The Siete Hermanos Trail is considered a highly challenging and serious trail run. It features significant elevation changes with relentlessly steep sections, some reaching grades of up to 25%. Many runners find themselves walking or power hiking the uphills due to its demanding nature.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña. Specific parking details for the Siete Hermanos Trail can vary, but generally, access points are available near the park's main visitor centers or designated trailheads. We recommend checking local park maps for the most precise parking locations.

faqs.question: Is the Siete Hermanos Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Regulations for dogs in Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña typically require them to be on a leash. Given the technical and challenging nature of this trail, ensure your dog is accustomed to rugged terrain and steep climbs if you plan to bring them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog the Siete Hermanos Trail depends on your preference. In winter, when foliage is sparse, you might enjoy more expansive scenic views from the ridge. However, be prepared for potentially colder temperatures and wet conditions. Spring and autumn offer milder weather, but the trail's technical aspects remain constant.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website or visitor center for any updated regulations or special event requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: What notable features or viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers scenic views from its ridge-top traverse. A specific highlight along this route is the Airplane Viewpoint, which provides panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: Does the Siete Hermanos Trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails within the park. You'll find sections that overlap with the Sendero de los Siete Hermanos, Transmurciana 19, Camino de la Cruz del Bajo Guadalentín, and Espubike. Tramo 4 Las Alquerías - El Berro, among others.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's technical and challenging nature, pack essentials like sturdy trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water, energy snacks, a first-aid kit, and appropriate layers of clothing. A map or GPS device is also highly recommended due to the intersecting trails.

faqs.question: Is the Siete Hermanos Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Due to its highly technical terrain, significant elevation changes, and overall demanding nature, the Siete Hermanos Trail is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It is best suited for experienced trail runners seeking a serious challenge.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodations near the trail?
faqs.answer: The Siete Hermanos Trail is located within the Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña. While the immediate trailhead might not have amenities, nearby villages such as El Berro or Gebas offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. It's advisable to check their operating hours in advance.
